project.name: clawx
project.description: ClawX is an Electron-based desktop application providing a graphical interface for OpenClaw AI agents, built with React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Vite, supporting macOS, Windows, and Linux.

nodes.name: context.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the HostApiContext interface that aggregates the four dependencies injected into every API route handler: GatewayManager, ClawHubService, HostEventBus, and the main BrowserWindow.

nodes.name: event-bus.ts
nodes.summary: Implements HostEventBus, an SSE (Server-Sent Events) fanout bus that tracks connected HTTP response streams and broadcasts named JSON events to all active renderer clients.

nodes.name: HostEventBus
nodes.summary: SSE fanout bus with addSseClient, emit, and closeAll methods; maintains a Set of active ServerResponse clients and removes them on close or write error.

nodes.name: route-utils.ts
nodes.summary: Provides shared HTTP utility helpers used across all route handlers: streaming JSON body parsing, CORS header injection, and JSON/no-content/text response senders.

nodes.name: parseJsonBody
nodes.summary: Async function that collects streaming IncomingMessage chunks, concatenates them, and JSON-parses the result, returning an empty object for empty bodies.

nodes.name: sendJson
nodes.summary: Sets CORS headers, assigns a status code, sets Content-Type to application/json, and ends the response with a JSON-stringified payload.

nodes.name: server.ts
nodes.summary: Creates and starts the Electron host API HTTP server, iterating a fixed list of route handlers for each request and falling back to a 404 JSON response; exports startHostApiServer.

nodes.name: startHostApiServer
nodes.summary: Creates a node:http server that fans requests through all registered RouteHandler functions and binds it to 127.0.0.1 on the configured CLAWX_HOST_API port.

nodes.name: agents.ts
nodes.summary: HTTP route handler for /api/agents CRUD operations; handles agent creation/renaming/deletion and channel assignment, including a cross-platform gateway process kill-and-respawn on agent deletion.

nodes.name: restartGatewayForAgentDeletion
nodes.summary: Force-kills the gateway process by PID or port (using SIGTERM/SIGKILL on Unix, taskkill on Windows, lsof on macOS/Linux) then calls gatewayManager.restart() to drop stale channel bot connections.

nodes.name: handleAgentRoutes
nodes.summary: Exported route handler for GET/POST /api/agents and nested PUT/DELETE endpoints for renaming agents and managing channel bindings.

nodes.name: app.ts
nodes.summary: Handles /api/events (SSE stream subscription with immediate status snapshot) and /api/app/openclaw-doctor (diagnose/fix mode) plus OPTIONS preflight.

nodes.name: handleAppRoutes
nodes.summary: Exported route handler that sets up SSE subscriptions, triggers openclaw-doctor diagnose/fix, and handles CORS preflight OPTIONS.

nodes.name: channels.ts
nodes.summary: Handles all channel-related API routes: listing configured channels and accounts, managing default accounts and agent bindings, WhatsApp login, config save/delete, and credential validation; triggers gateway reload or restart depending on channel type.

nodes.name: handleChannelRoutes
nodes.summary: Exported route handler covering 12+ channel API endpoints including binding management, config CRUD, WhatsApp login, and plugin install gating for dingtalk/wecom/qqbot/feishu.

nodes.name: buildChannelAccountsView
nodes.summary: Aggregates local config, gateway RPC status, and agent snapshot data to build a normalized ChannelAccountsView array for the UI, merging runtime and config-level account IDs.

nodes.name: cron.ts
nodes.summary: Handles cron job API routes (CRUD, toggle, trigger) via gateway RPC and cron session-history endpoint that reads JSONL run logs and sessions.json to reconstruct fallback chat messages.

nodes.name: handleCronRoutes
nodes.summary: Exported route handler for /api/cron/* endpoints: job CRUD/list/toggle/trigger via gateway RPC, plus session-history reconstruction from JSONL run logs.

nodes.name: buildCronSessionFallbackMessages
nodes.summary: Exported helper that transforms cron run log entries and job metadata into a sorted array of CronSessionFallbackMessage objects suitable for rendering in the chat UI when no live session transcript is available.

nodes.name: files.ts
nodes.summary: Handles file-related API routes for staging file paths or base64 buffers into the outbound media directory, generating image thumbnails and previews via Electron nativeImage, and saving images via a native dialog.

nodes.name: handleFileRoutes
nodes.summary: Exported route handler for file staging (paths and base64), thumbnail generation, and save-image-to-disk dialog endpoints.

nodes.name: gateway.ts
nodes.summary: Handles gateway control routes: info, status, health, start/stop/restart, control-UI URL, and the chat send-with-media RPC endpoint that encodes images as base64 attachments.

nodes.name: handleGatewayRoutes
nodes.summary: Exported route handler for gateway lifecycle (start/stop/restart/health/status) and the /api/chat/send-with-media endpoint that forwards messages with file attachments to the gateway via RPC.

nodes.name: logs.ts
nodes.summary: Handles /api/logs endpoints to retrieve recent log file contents, the log directory path, and a list of log files via the logger utility.

nodes.name: handleLogRoutes
nodes.summary: Exported route handler that serves log content (tail), log directory, and log file listing from the logger singleton.

nodes.name: providers.ts
nodes.summary: Handles provider account and legacy provider API routes: full CRUD for ProviderAccount objects, OAuth start/cancel/submit, API key validation, and runtime sync after each mutation.

nodes.name: handleProviderRoutes
nodes.summary: Exported route handler covering /api/provider-accounts (new) and /api/providers (legacy) CRUD, OAuth flows, and API key validation with runtime sync after every mutation.

nodes.name: sessions.ts
nodes.summary: Handles /api/sessions/delete which resolves a session's JSONL file path from sessions.json and renames it to .deleted.jsonl, removing the entry from the index.

nodes.name: handleSessionRoutes
nodes.summary: Exported route handler for POST /api/sessions/delete that resolves and renames session JSONL files and updates the sessions.json index.

nodes.name: settings.ts
nodes.summary: Handles application settings GET/PUT/reset routes with side effects: proxy reconfiguration triggers a gateway restart and launchAtStartup changes sync to the OS.

nodes.name: handleSettingsRoutes
nodes.summary: Exported route handler for /api/settings CRUD that triggers proxy sync and gateway restart when proxy keys change, and OS launch-at-startup sync when that setting changes.

nodes.name: skills.ts
nodes.summary: Handles skill config CRUD (/api/skills/*) and ClawHub marketplace routes (search, install, uninstall, list, open-readme, open-path) by delegating to ClawHubService.

nodes.name: handleSkillRoutes
nodes.summary: Exported route handler for skill config get/update and all ClawHub marketplace operations delegated to ctx.clawHubService.

nodes.name: usage.ts
nodes.summary: Handles /api/usage/recent-token-history GET with optional limit query param, delegating to getRecentTokenUsageHistory.

nodes.name: handleUsageRoutes
nodes.summary: Exported route handler returning paginated recent token usage history from the token-usage utility.

nodes.name: clawhub.ts
nodes.summary: Implements ClawHubService, a class that manages interactions with the ClawHub CLI for skills marketplace operations (search, explore, install, uninstall, list, openReadme/openPath) by spawning the CLI binary or running via Node.

nodes.name: ClawHubService
nodes.summary: Manages ClawHub CLI lifecycle: resolves CLI binary vs Node runner path, spawns child processes for search/explore/install/uninstall/list commands, strips ANSI from output, and resolves skill directories by manifest name for readme/path operations.

nodes.name: client.ts
nodes.summary: Typed HTTP/RPC client wrapper around GatewayManager exposing channel, skill, chat, cron, and provider operations as async methods. Defines the shared data model interfaces (Channel, Skill, CronTask, ProviderConfig, etc.) used across the gateway layer.

nodes.name: config-sync.ts
nodes.summary: Prepares the GatewayLaunchContext by syncing app settings, provider env vars, proxy config, and plugin upgrades before the gateway process is spawned. Auto-upgrades bundled channel plugins on startup.

nodes.name: connection-monitor.ts
nodes.summary: Implements ping/pong heartbeat monitoring for the gateway WebSocket connection, tracking consecutive misses and triggering timeout callbacks for reconnect logic.

nodes.name: event-dispatch.ts
nodes.summary: Routes raw gateway protocol events and JSON-RPC notifications to typed EventEmitter events (chat:message, channel:status, notification) consumed by the renderer process.

nodes.name: lifecycle-controller.ts
nodes.summary: Manages monotonically-incrementing lifecycle epochs to detect and reject stale async operations after a gateway restart or stop, throwing LifecycleSupersededError on epoch mismatch.

nodes.name: manager.ts
nodes.summary: Core GatewayManager orchestrates the full OpenClaw gateway process lifecycle: starting, stopping, restarting, WebSocket connection, health-checking, reconnect scheduling, and reload-on-config-change. Central hub that coordinates all gateway subsystems.

nodes.name: process-launcher.ts
nodes.summary: Spawns the OpenClaw gateway as an Electron utilityProcess with preloaded fetch patches and NODE_OPTIONS, resolving and rejecting a startup promise based on IPC ready/exit signals.

nodes.name: process-policy.ts
nodes.summary: Pure policy functions and types for reconnect scheduling decisions, lifecycle epoch management, and deferred-restart logic — dependency-free to enable unit testing without Electron mocks.

nodes.name: protocol.ts
nodes.summary: JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol type definitions (request, response, error, notification) plus gateway-specific event/error enums and factory functions for creating protocol messages.

nodes.name: reload-policy.ts
nodes.summary: Reads and parses the gateway reload policy (mode: hybrid/reload/restart/off, debounceMs) from the OpenClaw config file, providing validated defaults when absent or malformed.

nodes.name: request-store.ts
nodes.summary: In-memory store for pending JSON-RPC requests keyed by ID, with functions to resolve, reject, or clear all pending requests including clearing their timeouts.

nodes.name: restart-controller.ts
nodes.summary: Manages deferred restart state for the gateway: buffers restart requests during active startup or reconnect phases and flushes them once the lifecycle settles.

nodes.name: restart-governor.ts
nodes.summary: Circuit-breaker and rate-limiter for gateway restarts: tracks restart timestamps in a sliding window, applies exponential cooldown, and opens the circuit after too many consecutive failures.

nodes.name: startup-orchestrator.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the multi-attempt gateway startup sequence: tries connecting to an existing process first, falls back to spawning a new one, and triggers OpenClaw doctor repair on config failures.

nodes.name: startup-recovery.ts
nodes.summary: Dependency-free heuristics module for detecting invalid gateway config signals and transient start errors in stderr output, returning structured recovery action decisions.

nodes.name: startup-stderr.ts
nodes.summary: Classifies gateway process stderr lines into drop/debug/warn severity levels and maintains a capped rolling buffer of startup stderr output for diagnostics.

nodes.name: state.ts
nodes.summary: GatewayStateController maintains the current gateway status object, emitting status-change events and logging state transitions, acting as a single source of truth for gateway state.

nodes.name: supervisor.ts
nodes.summary: Gateway process supervision utilities: Python environment warmup, cross-platform process termination (including Windows process-tree kill and macOS launchctl unload), port-free waiting, and orphaned-process cleanup. Also runs the OpenClaw doctor repair subprocess.

nodes.name: ws-client.ts
nodes.summary: WebSocket client primitives for the Gateway: readiness probing via challenge-event detection, connection handshake with device-signed auth frames, and a retry loop that polls until the gateway is ready.

nodes.name: app-state.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal module exposing `isQuitting` / `setQuitting` accessors for the application quit flag, ensuring live-value reads across CJS bundler boundaries.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Electron main-process entry point: acquires single-instance locks, creates the BrowserWindow, initialises all subsystems (gateway, IPC, tray, updater, telemetry, skill/plugin installs, OAuth), and manages the full app lifecycle (quit, activate, second-instance focus).

nodes.name: ipc-handlers.ts
nodes.summary: Registers all IPC handlers between the renderer and main process across ~20 functional domains (gateway, providers, channels, settings, UV, logs, skills, cron, WhatsApp, OAuth, file staging, window controls). The central IPC surface of the application.

nodes.name: launch-at-startup.ts
nodes.summary: Cross-platform launch-at-startup logic: writes/removes a Linux `.desktop` autostart entry or calls Electron's `setLoginItemSettings` on macOS/Windows.

nodes.name: main-window-focus.ts
nodes.summary: State-machine helpers for handling second-instance focus requests: defers focus until the main window is ready-to-show if it hasn't appeared yet.

nodes.name: menu.ts
nodes.summary: Builds and sets the native application menu with File, Edit, View, Navigate, Window, and Help sections; uses IPC `navigate` messages to route to React pages.

nodes.name: process-instance-lock.ts
nodes.summary: File-based single-instance lock mechanism: creates a lock file, validates stale locks by checking PID liveness, and provides a release callback; supports both legacy (numeric PID) and structured JSON lock formats.

nodes.name: provider-model-sync.ts
nodes.summary: Builds `AgentProviderUpdatePayload` objects from ProviderConfig records for syncing non-OAuth provider API and model info to the OpenClaw runtime agent configuration.

nodes.name: proxy.ts
nodes.summary: Applies persisted proxy settings to Electron's default session and closes existing connections to force re-routing through the new proxy.

nodes.name: quit-lifecycle.ts
nodes.summary: Stateful quit lifecycle helpers: tracks whether gateway cleanup has started or completed and returns the appropriate `QuitLifecycleAction` to prevent duplicate or premature app.quit calls.

nodes.name: signal-quit.ts
nodes.summary: Factory for a POSIX signal handler (SIGINT/SIGTERM) that logs and delegates to `app.quit`.

nodes.name: tray.ts
nodes.summary: Creates and manages the system tray icon with a context menu (show/hide window, gateway status, quick navigation, check-for-updates, quit); handles click and double-click events.

nodes.name: updater.ts
nodes.summary: AppUpdater class (extends EventEmitter) wraps electron-updater with channel detection, auto-install countdown, and IPC handler registration; exposes checkForUpdates, downloadUpdate, quitAndInstall, and cancelAutoInstall.

nodes.name: window.ts
nodes.summary: Window state persistence utilities: saves/restores window bounds (position + size) via electron-store, validates that the saved position is visible on a connected display, and tracks state changes on resize/move/close.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Electron preload script: exposes a carefully whitelist-gated `electronAPI` object via contextBridge, providing IPC invoke/on/once/off methods and convenience helpers (openExternal, platform, isDev) to the sandboxed renderer.

nodes.name: warmupManagedPythonReadiness
nodes.summary: Non-blocking startup check that probes Python environment readiness and triggers a background repair via setupManagedPython if the environment is missing or incomplete.

nodes.name: terminateOwnedGatewayProcess
nodes.summary: Terminates the Electron-owned gateway UtilityProcess gracefully with a 5-second timeout, using `taskkill /T` on Windows and SIGKILL fallback on Unix.

nodes.name: unloadLaunchctlGatewayService
nodes.summary: macOS-only: unloads the `ai.openclaw.gateway` launchctl service via `launchctl bootout` and removes the legacy plist file to prevent auto-respawn.

nodes.name: waitForPortFree
nodes.summary: Polls a TCP port until it is available for binding (no listener), with configurable timeout; used to wait for Windows TCP TIME_WAIT to clear after process kill.

nodes.name: findExistingGatewayProcess
nodes.summary: Detects and terminates orphaned gateway processes on the target port, then probes whether a valid gateway WebSocket is accessible; returns port info if found.

nodes.name: runOpenClawDoctorRepair
nodes.summary: Forks the OpenClaw entry script with `doctor --fix --yes --non-interactive` args as a UtilityProcess with a 120-second timeout to repair the OpenClaw installation.

nodes.name: probeGatewayReady
nodes.summary: Opens a WebSocket to the gateway and returns true only when the `connect.challenge` event is received, distinguishing a truly ready gateway from a TCP-open-but-not-ready state.

nodes.name: waitForGatewayReady
nodes.summary: Retry loop (default 2400 attempts × 200ms) that calls probeGatewayReady, aborting early if the gateway process exits before becoming ready.

nodes.name: buildGatewayConnectFrame
nodes.summary: Constructs the JSON WebSocket `connect` request frame including device identity signature, scopes, protocol version, and client metadata.

nodes.name: connectGatewaySocket
nodes.summary: Full gateway WebSocket connection: waits for `connect.challenge`, signs and sends the connect frame, registers pending request handlers, and resolves the Promise with the authenticated WebSocket.

nodes.name: isQuitting
nodes.summary: Returns the current application quit flag value as a live accessor to avoid CJS bundler snapshot issues.

nodes.name: setQuitting
nodes.summary: Sets the application quit flag to true (or a provided boolean), signalling that the app is in the process of quitting.

nodes.name: initialize
nodes.summary: Async main initialisation: sets up logger, telemetry, proxy, menu, window, tray, IPC handlers, host API server, updater, event bridges, gateway auto-start, context merging, and CLI/skill/plugin installation.

nodes.name: createWindow
nodes.summary: Creates the main BrowserWindow with platform-appropriate title bar, context isolation, and sandbox settings; loads the Vite dev URL or production HTML.

nodes.name: createMainWindow
nodes.summary: Wraps createWindow with ready-to-show and close event handlers: honours pending second-instance focus state and hides to tray on close instead of destroying.

nodes.name: registerIpcHandlers
nodes.summary: Top-level IPC registration dispatcher that delegates to ~20 domain-specific register* sub-functions covering the entire main↔renderer communication surface.

nodes.name: applyLaunchAtStartupSetting
nodes.summary: Applies the launch-at-startup setting cross-platform: writes/removes a Linux .desktop autostart file or calls setLoginItemSettings on macOS/Windows.

nodes.name: syncLaunchAtStartupSettingFromStore
nodes.summary: Reads the `launchAtStartup` setting from the persistent store and applies it via applyLaunchAtStartupSetting.

nodes.name: createMainWindowFocusState

nodes.name: requestSecondInstanceFocus
nodes.summary: Handles focus request from a second app instance: returns 'focus-now' if window exists, or sets pending flag and returns 'defer' if window is not yet ready.

nodes.name: createMenu
nodes.summary: Builds the native Electron application menu template (File, Edit, View, Navigate, Window, Help) and sets it as the application menu; sends IPC navigate messages for page routing.

nodes.name: acquireProcessInstanceFileLock
nodes.summary: Attempts to exclusively create a lock file containing the current PID; handles stale locks from dead processes by deleting and retrying; returns acquired status, lock path, and a release callback.

nodes.name: buildNonOAuthAgentProviderUpdate
nodes.summary: Constructs an AgentProviderUpdatePayload from a ProviderConfig for non-OAuth, non-custom, non-ollama providers to sync API and model info to the runtime.

nodes.name: applyProxySettings
nodes.summary: Reads proxy settings from the store (or accepts a partial override), builds the Electron proxy config, applies it to the default session, and resets all connections.

nodes.name: createQuitLifecycleState
nodes.summary: Factory that creates a fresh QuitLifecycleState with both cleanup flags set to false.

nodes.name: requestQuitLifecycleAction
nodes.summary: State-machine step: returns 'allow-quit' if cleanup is done, 'cleanup-in-progress' if already started, or 'start-cleanup' (and sets cleanupStarted) if this is the first call.

nodes.name: createSignalQuitHandler
nodes.summary: Returns a POSIX signal handler closure that logs the received signal and delegates to the provided requestQuit hook.

nodes.name: createTray
nodes.summary: Creates the system tray icon with platform-specific icon selection, tooltip, context menu, and click/double-click handlers for show/hide behaviour.

nodes.name: updateTrayStatus
nodes.summary: Updates the tray tooltip text to reflect the current gateway status string.

nodes.name: destroyTray
nodes.summary: Destroys the tray icon and nullifies the reference, used on app quit.

nodes.name: getWindowState
nodes.summary: Retrieves the persisted window bounds from electron-store and resets the position if it falls outside all connected display bounds.

nodes.name: saveWindowState
nodes.summary: Persists the current BrowserWindow bounds (or just the maximized flag) to electron-store.

nodes.name: trackWindowState
nodes.summary: Attaches resize, move, and close listeners to a BrowserWindow to automatically save state on each event.

nodes.name: AppUpdater
nodes.summary: Wraps electron-updater with channel-aware feed URL configuration, auto-install countdown logic, renderer-facing IPC forwarding, and an EventEmitter interface for status changes.

nodes.name: ProcessInstanceFileLock
nodes.summary: Interface describing the result of acquireProcessInstanceFileLock: acquired flag, lock path, ownerPid/ownerFormat for diagnostics, and a release function.

nodes.name: MainWindowFocusState
nodes.summary: Interface holding the `pendingSecondInstanceFocus` boolean flag used by the focus-state helpers.

nodes.name: QuitLifecycleState
nodes.summary: Interface holding `cleanupStarted` and `cleanupCompleted` boolean flags used by the quit-lifecycle state machine.

nodes.name: SignalQuitHandlerHooks

nodes.label: runtime-event-handlers.ts
nodes.summary: Handles Gateway runtime event state machine transitions for the chat store. Processes 'started', 'delta', 'final', 'error', 'aborted', and unknown states. On final tool-result events, snapshots the streaming assistant message into messages[] to preserve intermediate thinking+tool turns. On error, keeps 'sending:true' for a 15-second grace period to allow Gateway retries before finalizing failure.
nodes.name: runtime-event-handlers.ts

nodes.label: handleRuntimeEventState
nodes.summary: Switch-based handler for all Gateway chat event states (started/delta/final/error/aborted). Manages streaming message accumulation, tool status upserts, image attachment collection from tool results, and error recovery timer.
nodes.name: handleRuntimeEventState

nodes.label: runtime-send-actions.ts
nodes.summary: Creates sendMessage and abortRun actions for the chat store. sendMessage resolves the correct session for the target agent, adds an optimistic user message, starts a history poll (every 4s after 3s delay) and a 90-second safety timeout, then calls gateway:rpc 'chat.send' or 'chat:sendWithMedia' IPC. abortRun calls 'chat.abort' RPC and clears all streaming state.
nodes.name: runtime-send-actions.ts

nodes.label: createRuntimeSendActions
nodes.summary: Factory returning sendMessage and abortRun actions. sendMessage implements agent-targeted session routing, optimistic message insertion, polling and safety timeout management, and media-aware IPC dispatch.
nodes.name: createRuntimeSendActions

nodes.label: runtime-ui-actions.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal factory for UI-only chat store actions: toggleThinking (flips showThinking), refresh (reloads history + sessions in parallel), and clearError (nullifies error field).

nodes.label: createRuntimeUiActions
nodes.summary: Returns toggleThinking, refresh, and clearError action implementations for the chat Zustand store.
nodes.name: createRuntimeUiActions

nodes.label: session-actions.ts
nodes.summary: Creates session management actions: loadSessions (fetches sessions.list RPC, deduplicates, resolves canonical keys, background-labels non-main sessions), switchSession (clears streaming state, removes empty ghost sessions), newSession (generates timestamped key, cleans up empty predecessor), deleteSession (soft-deletes via session:delete IPC), and cleanupEmptySession.
nodes.name: session-actions.ts

nodes.summary: Factory returning loadSessions, switchSession, newSession, deleteSession, and cleanupEmptySession. Implements canonical session key deduplication and ghost-session cleanup logic.

nodes.label: store-api.ts
nodes.summary: Type definitions for the chat store's functional API. Defines ChatSet, ChatGet function types, SessionHistoryActions (loadSessions/switchSession/newSession/deleteSession/cleanupEmptySession/loadHistory), and RuntimeActions (sendMessage/abortRun/handleChatEvent/toggleThinking/refresh/clearError).
nodes.name: store-api.ts

nodes.label: types.ts
nodes.summary: Core type definitions for the chat store. Defines AttachedFileMeta (local file metadata), RawMessage (OpenClaw chat.history message with optional _attachedFiles), ContentBlock (text/image/thinking/tool_use/tool_result union), ChatSession, ToolStatus, and ChatState (full Zustand state shape with default constants).
nodes.name: types.ts

nodes.label: cron.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store for scheduled cron jobs. Manages jobs[], loading, and error state. Implements fetchJobs (GET /api/cron/jobs), createJob (POST), updateJob (PUT, optimistic update), deleteJob (DELETE), toggleJob (POST /api/cron/toggle), triggerJob (POST /api/cron/trigger with follow-up refresh), and setJobs.
nodes.name: cron.ts

nodes.label: gateway.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store managing Gateway process lifecycle and event routing. init() subscribes to SSE events (gateway:status, gateway:error, gateway:notification, gateway:chat-message, gateway:channel-status) with deduplication (30s TTL map). Provides start/stop/restart/checkHealth/rpc methods. Routes agent-phase notifications to useChatStore and channel-status updates to useChannelsStore via dynamic import().
nodes.name: gateway.ts

nodes.label: providers.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store for AI provider/account management. Wraps refreshProviderSnapshot (fetches statuses, accounts, vendors, defaultAccountId in one call) and exposes CRUD operations for providers and accounts via /api/providers and /api/provider-accounts. Provides legacy aliases (addProvider, updateProvider, deleteProvider, etc.) for backward compatibility.
nodes.name: providers.ts

nodes.label: settings.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand persisted store ('clawx-settings') for application settings. Covers theme, language (resolved via resolveSupportedLanguage), gateway port/autostart, proxy configuration, update channel, and UI state. init() syncs from /api/settings; setter actions for gateway/launch/telemetry/devMode immediately POST to /api/settings/<field>.
nodes.name: settings.ts

nodes.label: skills.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store for skill/plugin management. fetchSkills merges data from three sources: Gateway RPC 'skills.status', /api/clawhub/list (installed on disk), and /api/skills/configs (Electron config). Implements search (ClawHub marketplace), install/uninstall (ClawHub API), enable/disable (Gateway RPC 'skills.update'). Maps error codes to i18n error keys.
nodes.name: skills.ts

nodes.label: update.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store for app update management. init() fetches current version/status via IPC, subscribes to 'update:status-changed' and 'update:auto-install-countdown' IPC events, and auto-checks on startup (after 10s delay). Provides checkForUpdates (30s timeout), downloadUpdate, installUpdate, cancelAutoInstall, setChannel, and setAutoDownload.
nodes.name: update.ts

layers.name: Electron Main Process
layers.description: Orchestrates the Electron application lifecycle for ClawX: app startup, BrowserWindow creation, system tray, auto-updater, IPC handler registration, proxy configuration, quit lifecycle, single-instance lock, and login-item management.

layers.name: Host API Server
layers.description: Implements the localhost HTTP API server in ClawX that exposes REST endpoints (agents, channels, cron, files, gateway, logs, providers, sessions, settings, skills, usage) consumed by the React renderer via fetch calls.

layers.name: OpenClaw Gateway Management
layers.description: Manages the full lifecycle of the OpenClaw AI agent gateway subprocess in ClawX: process launch, supervisor, WebSocket client, config sync, connection monitoring, restart governor, reload policy, event dispatch, and startup/recovery orchestration.

layers.name: Backend Services
layers.description: Provides the backend business-logic services in ClawX's main process: provider lifecycle management (migration, runtime sync, validation, store), secrets storage, shared provider registry and type definitions.

layers.name: Electron Utilities
layers.description: Cross-cutting utility modules for ClawX's main process: config file I/O, OAuth flows (browser, device, Gemini, OpenAI Codex, WhatsApp), OpenClaw CLI/auth/proxy/doctor/workspace helpers, UV environment setup, paths, proxy, secure storage, token usage tracking, telemetry, and plugin installation.

layers.name: React UI Layer
layers.description: Implements all React renderer-process UI in ClawX: the root App component, page views (Chat, Cron, Models, Settings, Setup, Skills), feature components (ChannelConfigModal, ProvidersSettings, UpdateSettings), shared common components (ErrorBoundary, FeedbackState, LoadingSpinner, StatusBadge), Radix-based primitive UI components, and static provider assets.

layers.name: State Management
layers.description: Contains all Zustand stores and React hooks for ClawX's renderer process: chat runtime/session/history/event action slices, agents, channels, cron, gateway, providers, settings, skills, and update stores, plus custom hooks for loading and scroll behavior.

layers.name: Frontend Utilities
layers.description: Provides the renderer-side utility layer for ClawX: the Host API client and IPC bridge (host-api, host-events, api-client), the error-model normalizer, TypeScript type definitions (agent, channel, cron, gateway, skill), and the shared language constant used by both processes.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: All Vitest unit tests and test infrastructure for ClawX, covering gateway lifecycle, provider management, chat store actions, API routes, Electron utilities, and renderer components.

layers.name: Build & Configuration
layers.description: All build tooling, packaging scripts, and project-level configuration files for ClawX: electron-builder after-pack hook, OpenClaw/plugin/skills bundle scripts, comms baseline/compare/replay scripts, asset generation, UV/Node downloader, Linux post-install hooks, Vite/Vitest/Tailwind/PostCSS/ESLint configs, and ad-hoc test scripts.

tour.title: Electron Main Process Entry Point
tour.description: electron/main/index.ts is the outermost shell of ClawX: it acquires a single-instance lock so only one copy of the app runs, creates the BrowserWindow, and then bootstraps every major subsystem in sequence — GatewayManager, IPC handlers, system tray, auto-updater, OAuth helpers, skill/plugin installers, and telemetry. Understanding this file gives you the startup chronology and the wiring between all the layers you will visit next. It also handles the full quit sequence, macOS 'activate', and second-instance focus. With 22 outgoing imports it is the highest fan-out node in the graph and the natural BFS root.
tour.languageLesson: Electron enforces a process boundary between the Node.js 'main' process and the Chromium 'renderer' process. index.ts lives entirely on the main side and cannot touch the DOM; the renderer cannot call Node APIs directly. All communication flows through IPC.

tour.title: Renderer-Main Bridge: Preload and IPC Surface
tour.description: electron/preload/index.ts runs in a privileged Node context inside the renderer sandbox and uses contextBridge to expose a whitelist-gated window.electronAPI object — the only channel the React UI can use to reach the main process. The companion file electron/main/ipc-handlers.ts registers the other side of every IPC channel: roughly 20 functional domains including gateway control, provider management, OAuth flows, settings, logs, cron, and window controls. Together these two files define the complete public API surface between the renderer and the main process.
tour.languageLesson: contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld(name, api) is Electron's safe way to pass objects from the preload script to the renderer without enabling full Node access. The exposed object is deeply cloned on the boundary so the renderer cannot hold a reference to Node objects.

tour.title: Host API Server: the Localhost HTTP Bridge
tour.description: In addition to IPC, ClawX runs a localhost HTTP server (electron/api/server.ts) that the React renderer hits with ordinary fetch() calls — no IPC invoke needed. The server iterates a fixed list of route-handler modules for every inbound request. electron/api/context.ts defines the HostApiContext interface that injects four dependencies into every handler: GatewayManager, ClawHubService, HostEventBus, and the BrowserWindow. electron/api/event-bus.ts implements an SSE fanout bus that pushes real-time events (chat messages, gateway status, notifications) to all connected renderer tabs without polling. electron/api/route-utils.ts provides shared CORS, JSON parsing, and response helpers used by every route.
tour.languageLesson: Using a plain localhost HTTP server alongside Electron IPC is an intentional architectural choice: it lets the React renderer use familiar fetch/SWR patterns instead of ipcRenderer.invoke, and makes each API route independently testable as plain HTTP.

tour.title: Gateway Manager: the OpenClaw Subprocess Orchestrator
tour.description: electron/gateway/manager.ts (990 lines, fan-out 11, highest fan-in among gateway files) is the central coordinator for the OpenClaw AI agent process. It delegates to a constellation of focused submodules: process-launcher.ts spawns the subprocess via Electron utilityProcess.fork(); startup-orchestrator.ts implements the multi-attempt startup sequence (connect to existing → spawn new → doctor repair); supervisor.ts handles Python warmup, cross-platform process-tree kill, and orphan cleanup; restart-governor.ts is a circuit-breaker that applies exponential back-off after repeated failures; state.ts maintains the single authoritative gateway status. The manager itself is bidirectionally coupled with state.ts (the only bidirectional pair in the graph), reflecting their tight co-ownership of lifecycle state.
tour.languageLesson: Electron utilityProcess.fork() runs a Node.js script in a sandboxed child process with its own IPC channel but without Chromium overhead. It is the preferred way to run heavy or crash-prone backend work (like the OpenClaw Python gateway wrapper) without risking the main window.

tour.title: Gateway WebSocket Layer: Connection, Events, and Config Sync
tour.description: Once the subprocess is alive, GatewayManager establishes a WebSocket connection (ws-client.ts) using a device-signed auth handshake and challenge-event readiness probing. connection-monitor.ts runs a ping/pong heartbeat and fires reconnect callbacks after consecutive misses. event-dispatch.ts receives raw JSON-RPC notifications from the gateway and re-emits them as typed EventEmitter events (chat:message, channel:status, notification) that the renderer consumes via SSE. Before each startup, config-sync.ts snapshots app settings, provider env vars, proxy config, and plugin versions into a GatewayLaunchContext passed to the subprocess so it starts with the correct configuration.
tour.languageLesson: gateway/client.ts wraps GatewayManager with a typed HTTP/RPC facade. This adapter pattern keeps the rest of the codebase from importing GatewayManager directly and means the gateway communication protocol can evolve without touching every call site.

tour.title: Provider and Model Management
tour.description: ClawX supports multiple AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, OpenRouter, and more). The type system lives in electron/shared/providers/types.ts (ProviderType, ProviderAccount, ModelSummary) and the static built-in registry in electron/shared/providers/registry.ts with lookup helpers for env vars, backend configs, and UI metadata. electron/services/providers/provider-service.ts is the singleton service layer that orchestrates CRUD, secret storage (secret-store.ts), and OpenClaw auth-profile sync. provider-runtime-sync.ts listens for store change events and pushes API key and token updates to the live gateway runtime without requiring a restart. electron/api/routes/providers.ts exposes this as a REST API including OAuth start/cancel/submit flows.
tour.languageLesson: Separating shared/providers/types.ts (pure types, no Electron imports) from services/providers/ (Electron runtime) makes the type layer importable in both main and renderer without bundler complications, a common pattern in Electron TypeScript projects.

tour.title: Channel and Agent Configuration
tour.description: Channels are the messaging integrations ClawX connects to (Telegram, WhatsApp, DingTalk, etc.). electron/utils/channel-config.ts owns the full read/write lifecycle for openclaw.yml, managing plugin allowlists, per-channel credential validation, and multi-account bindings. electron/utils/agent-config.ts handles the companion agents.yml file: agent create/rename/delete, channel-to-agent binding, and workspace directory provisioning. The HTTP surface in electron/api/routes/channels.ts routes mutations through these utilities and triggers either a gateway reload (soft config push) or a full restart depending on the change type. This interplay between the YAML config files and the live gateway is the central operational loop ClawX manages on the user's behalf.

tour.title: OpenClaw Auth and Workspace Sync
tour.description: electron/utils/openclaw-auth.ts is the deepest integration point between ClawX and the OpenClaw Gateway: it reads and writes auth-profiles.json across every configured agent directory, translates ClawX ProviderConfig records into OpenClaw environment variables, handles OAuth token injection, default model setting, and config sanitization (including Moonshot special-casing and compaction mode normalization). electron/utils/openclaw-workspace.ts manages the workspace context files (AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md) that OpenClaw reads at startup, repairing ClawX-owned sections delimited by HTML comment markers. Together they ensure the gateway always boots with a consistent, up-to-date view of the user's providers and workspace.

tour.title: Cross-Cutting Utilities: Paths, Config, Logger, and Secrets
tour.description: Four utility modules underpin the entire electron side of ClawX. electron/utils/paths.ts (fan-in 9, third most-imported file) resolves every important filesystem path — OpenClaw config/skills/data directories, ClawX logs, packaged binary locations, and win-shell helpers — in a single place, making the rest of the codebase path-agnostic. electron/utils/config.ts exports PORTS, APP_PATHS, UPDATE_CONFIG, and GATEWAY_CONFIG constants that all subsystems share. electron/utils/logger.ts (fan-in 19, the most-imported file in the graph) is the async-buffered rotating log writer with a 500-entry in-memory ring buffer exposed to the UI. electron/services/secrets/secret-store.ts provides the ElectronStoreSecretStore abstraction that keeps provider API keys out of plain config files.
tour.languageLesson: The logger's 19-node fan-in (highest in the graph) and paths.ts's 9-node fan-in are signals that these modules are architectural load-bearers. High fan-in files are the most impactful to change carefully and the most valuable to understand early in a codebase tour.

tour.title: Plugin Installation and ClawHub Skills Marketplace
tour.description: ClawX ships bundled chat plugins (DingTalk, WeCom, Feishu, QQBot) that it auto-installs into the OpenClaw skills directory at startup. electron/utils/plugin-install.ts performs version-aware copying from app resources, patches plugin manifest IDs, handles Windows extended-path normalization, and exposes per-channel and batch install functions called from the main entry point before the gateway starts. The skills marketplace is served by electron/gateway/clawhub.ts (ClawHubService), which wraps the ClawHub CLI binary to expose search, install, uninstall, and explore operations as typed async methods, with the HTTP surface in electron/api/routes/skills.ts. The updater (electron/main/updater.ts) rounds out the lifecycle by wrapping electron-updater with channel detection, auto-install countdown, and IPC handler registration for the Settings UI.
